data.body:
SECURITY / OSS-ALIGNMENT ASSESSMENT — identity (shipped, e68d) + op-cert plan (sonic-columned-sturgeon). Grounded in ADR 0044/0045 + attest/ code; benchmarked against gittuf, TUF, Sigstore, in-toto/SLSA, git SSH-signing, RFC 8725, NIST SSDF (cited, 2025-2026 actively-maintained).

VERDICT: Well-aligned — textbook-conformant in several respects; on the standout mechanism (log-position key validity) it INDEPENDENTLY CONVERGED on gittuf's frontier design (NDSS 2025 Distinguished Paper; OpenSSF Incubating). Gaps are real but bounded — mostly hardening/docs plus ONE verification item worth prioritising.

STRONG ALIGNMENT:
- DSSE sign-exact-bytes + never-re-serialize + keyid-is-unauthenticated-hint = LITERALLY the DSSE spec's own MUST requirements. Textbook.
- Pinned per-kind scheme table, verifier never reads scheme from record = structural form of RFC 8725 anti-alg-confusion, generalised to "bind kind to one scheme+key-source".
- In-toto Statement subject binding {event_uuid, content_hash} = in-toto/SLSA subject.digest anti-replay pattern.
- Commit-ancestry key validity (added_at_commit/revoked_at_commit via merge-base --is-ancestor) = gittuf RSL log-position validity. Both DELIBERATELY drop TUF wall-clock expiry + timestamp role (gittuf #280): append-only log position beats a signed clock in a git-backed system. Peer-reviewed direction; rebar not an outlier.
- TOFU genesis + signed rotation = TUF (out-of-band root then in-band signed rotation) + gittuf (TOFU first policy).
- Merge-gate re-verification over merged log = correct answer to GitHub's KNOWN limitation (per-commit Verified is point-in-time + non-retroactive; revocation never bites history). Re-verify-on-read makes revocation enforce.
- Op-cert "trusted rebar-mcp fetches authoritative state itself" = SLSA Build L3 trusted control plane + GitHub OIDC-populated provenance (claims set by provider, not workload).
- Two authority tiers (person/TOFU/low vs environment/pinned-out-of-band/high) = Sigstore human-vs-workload + SLSA builder identity.

GAPS (ranked):
1. ANTI-ROLLBACK/MONOTONICITY (highest, VERIFY). gittuf shipped the SAME model and STILL had a rollback CVE (CVE-2026-44544) until v0.14.0 added a monotonic policy counter; TUF hard-codes strict N+1 root versioning. rebar's 'a new event descends the revocation commit so a revoked key fails not-descends-revoked_at_commit' appears to defeat the DIRECT reuse case. BUT load-bearing assumption = tickets-branch history integrity: that branch is AUTO-PUSHED and NOT Gerrit-gated, and merge-base --is-ancestor trusts it is not force-pushed/reordered. rebar's analog of gittuf's 'RSL push access' threat. RECOMMEND explicit anti-rollback invariant + regression test (revoked-then-re-introduced-at-later-position must FAIL) + documented tickets-branch-rewrite threat analysis.
2. NO TRANSPARENCY LOG (biggest divergence from Sigstore/Rekor). PREVENTION (pinned keys, fail-closed) but no monitorable witness -> no third-party DETECTION of key/identity misuse (Rekor Monitor catches real malicious releases). Merged-log is a partial self-referential substitute. Acceptable for low-authority TOFU authorship; for HIGH-authority op-cert env key consider bundled inclusion-proof/witness or KEY_ADD monitoring. Document residual risk.
3. TOFU first-use risk (authorship). MITM'd first key trusted thereafter; mitigated by low authority + DAG anchoring; Sigstore sidesteps via OIDC. Make explicit in identity.md.
4. NO THRESHOLD/role separation. TUF+gittuf use thresholds for high-authority roots; rebar single key per identity/env -> one compromised key signs anything. For op-cert required-env keys evaluate threshold or documented compromise runbook. Out-of-band pinned root mitigates.
5. PROVENANCE != CORRECTNESS (shared). Valid op-cert attests state came from trusted fetcher, not that work is correct. State explicitly.

RECS: R1 anti-rollback invariant+test & tickets-branch-rewrite analysis (candidate bug/investigation). R2 op-cert ADR bundled-proof/witness or KEY_ADD monitoring + document transparency-log out-of-scope. R3 op-cert threshold OR compromise runbook. R4 identity.md TOFU risk + recovery limits + provenance!=correctness. R5 pin cited spec versions/SHAs in op-cert ADR.

Sources: gittuf design-doc + NDSS 2025 + CVE-2026-44544; TUF v1.0.31; DSSE protocol.md; in-toto Statement v1; slsa.dev build-provenance L3; GitHub attestations + sig-verification docs; RFC 8725; NIST SP 800-218.
